name: Ottawa (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the Ottawa, Rideau and Gatineau rivers and the waterways of the region
the green spaces and Greenbelt that surround the city
the stylised letter 'O' representing the new amalgamated city

description: A vertical bicolour with the hoist half blue and the fly half green, charged in the centre with a stylised white letter 'O'.
history: Adopted following the 2001 amalgamation of the Regional Municipality of Ottawa–Carleton's eleven constituent municipalities into the new single City of Ottawa. The Ottawa Transition Board approved the design on 23 October 2000 and the new City Council formally adopted the flag on 24 January 2001, on which date it was first flown. It replaced the city's previous purple-red-blue tricolour adopted in 1901 — at the time of its retirement the oldest municipal flag in Canada.

features.symbolism: blue at the hoist for the rivers (Ottawa, Rideau, Gatineau) and waterways; green at the fly for the city's green spaces and Greenbelt
a white stylised 'O' representing the vibrancy and forward movement of the new amalgamated City of Ottawa
